section.heading: Sense of Congress regarding protecting Afghanistan’s President
section.chapeau: It is the sense of Congress that—

paragraph.content: any United States physical protection force provided for the personal security of the President of Afghanistan should be composed of United States diplomatic security, law-enforcement, or military personnel, and should not utilize private contracted personnel to provide actual physical protection services;
United States allies should be invited to volunteer active-duty military or law enforcement personnel to participate in such a protection force; and
such a protection force should be limited in duration and should be succeeded by qualified Afghan security forces as soon as practicable.
